operator_request: Create a new starting design that merges all peer reviews into the original documentation, improves the yt-dlp-derived architecture, enforces the architecture and good coding habits through build rules, keeps the independent diagnostic watcher current, and distills Ponytail critiques and YAGNI research into useful project heuristics.

preserve: Complete expanded Ferric Forager feature scope
FFmpeg and ffprobe as supervised external processes
yt-dlp as pinned oracle and source research rather than production dependency
change: Resolve review findings into explicit contracts and phase gates
Replace premature fine-grained crate topology with coarse enforceable architecture
Add executable architecture and coding-quality enforcement requirements
non_goals: Silent narrowing for an easier parity claim

assumptions: The intended Ponytail project is verified as DietrichGebert/ponytail and research is pinned to commit 16f29800fd2681bdf24f3eb4ccffe38be3baec6b.
The new v0.3.0 file becomes the design source only after authority paths and validators are updated together.
YAGNI limits speculative mechanisms, not known compatibility scope or mandatory correctness controls.

sources.evidence: Complete architecture/performance, compatibility/product, security/reliability, synthesis, and readiness artifacts, including pinned yt-dlp, FFmpeg, Tokio, transport, JavaScript, storage, concurrency, fuzzing, and field-implementation research.

sources.source: https://github.com/DietrichGebert/ponytail at commit 16f29800fd2681bdf24f3eb4ccffe38be3baec6b and its pinned skills/benchmarks
sources.evidence: Current reuse/simplification ladder, overengineering-review limits, corrected agentic benchmark, and disclosed limitations.
Inflated-baseline correction, independent benchmark with robustness losses on unstated edge cases, and open scope-before-simplicity hardening proposal.

Independent non-peer-reviewed reproduction found occasional missing persistence, types, tests, and guards; unmerged draft proposes invariant-owner root-cause fixes while preserving verification, recovery, cleanup, and affected callers. Both are critique evidence, not authority.
YAGNI applies to presumptive capability complexity; refactoring, self-testing code, and continuous delivery are enabling practices.

sources.source: Rust and Cargo official documentation plus cargo-deny, RustSec, Loom, Miri, cargo-hack, cargo-udeps, and cargo-mutants primary repositories
sources.evidence: Workspace metadata, lint inheritance, Clippy limitations, deterministic locked builds, dependency policy, model/unsafe/feature/mutation check scopes and limitations.

sources.source: systemd watchdog, Kubernetes probe, Prometheus absent-signal, and gRPC health primary documentation
sources.evidence: Progress-sensitive liveness, startup/readiness separation, absent-data detection, and serving-state patterns without adopting their runtimes.

sources.source: Hugging Face search, Civitai search, Reddit discussions, and X/Twitter search
sources.evidence: Hugging Face returned adjacent agent-architecture material but no Ferric-specific or Ponytail-authoritative implementation; Civitai and X produced no relevant technical evidence; Reddit supplied lower-grade qualitative concerns about code golf, readability, and missed edge handling and was used only as critique corroboration.

patterns_found: Coarse inward dependency rings plus machine-checkable Cargo metadata edges prevent premature crate topology and architecture drift.
YAGNI is safe only after scope proof and with refactoring, tests, CI, and explicit carve-outs for correctness and risk controls.
LOC reduction is not quality evidence and can reward omitted persistence, types, guards, or tests.
Watcher freshness has distinct runtime-progress, wire/schema, independent capability inventory, synthetic corpus, current-binary replay, cross-version, privacy, and paired-artifact contracts.
Current-binary replay is necessary but insufficient by itself; independent inventory and canonical/synthetic corpora prevent producer/watcher self-agreement from hiding omissions.
Established tools should provide narrow evidence while one governed wrapper preserves reproducible commands and reports.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse the existing typed event, invariance, backpressure, termination, process-independence, task-packet, and review-gate authority rather than create a parallel quality system.
Reuse Cargo metadata and workspace lint inheritance for architecture and coding enforcement.
Reuse the v0.2.0 design verbatim as the preservation base and integrate review resolutions in place.
Reuse the diagnostic contract for both producer/watcher compile-time coverage and independent golden replay fixtures.
rejected_options: Install or copy Ponytail wholesale as always-on project authority.
Use one-line, LOC, diff-size, file-count, or abstraction-count reduction as an acceptance target.
Ban every single-implementation trait, including current process/security/network/storage/test boundaries.
Freeze the v0.2.0 approximately thirty-crate workspace before contract and ownership evidence.
Treat cargo-deny, Clippy, audit, Miri, Loom, fuzzing, or tests as proof of absence of arbitrary defects.
Prove watcher freshness through timestamps, touch requirements, or process existence.
Make watcher acknowledgements part of Ferric correctness or success.
research.selected_approach: Preservation-first v0.3.0 design with a Phase 0 bootstrap closure, coarse contracts/engine/adapters/composition rings, reusable facade and Handshake adapter, machine-readable Cargo architecture policy constrained by a separate exception allowlist, built-in repository-root Cargo verification commands, three-gate scope-before-simplicity YAGNI evidence, and a process-independent watcher proven through bounded degraded lifecycle, typed loop state, independent inventory, canonical/synthetic corpora, current-binary replay, paired artifacts, privacy checks, and fault tests.
risks: The new design can look enforceable before the Cargo workspace, architecture checker, and watcher suite exist.
Strict linting can cause meaningless blanket exceptions or toolchain-update churn.
Shared producer/watcher protocol code can reproduce the same serialization bug on both sides.
YAGNI can be misused to narrow mandatory compatibility or reliability work.
The expanded v0.3.0 design may preserve prose while still missing one review finding.
A bounded independent watcher cannot guarantee absolute crash evidence when every persistence path is unavailable.
A product-owned architecture policy could self-authorize exceptions unless governance supplies a separate allowlist.
mitigations: Mark every build gate REQUIRED_NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED, authorize only the minimal Phase 0 bootstrap and bounded spikes needed to make them executable, and retain broad feature implementation as blocked.
Pin the toolchain, select restriction lints individually, require stable-ID exception reasons, and validate exception scope.
Keep independent golden bytes/schema validation and cross-version replay in addition to shared types.
Put scope/correctness/risk review before simplification and make non-negotiable carve-outs normative.
Validate all 47 SYN/AP/FCP/SR finding IDs against an explicit disposition table.
Scope critical-event guarantees to post-admission healthy-storage writes, reserve critical capacity, expose residual gaps, and never block Ferric correctness.
Require every architecture exception to cite the separate canonical build-rule allowlist and split graph, source, runtime, packaging, and artifact proof classes.
validation_plan: Parse every changed YAML/JSON authority and state file.
Validate contiguous FF-BUILD IDs and registered FF-GATE IDs.
Validate frontmatter/topic structure, original top-level section preservation, core scope markers, and all 47 finding rows.
Validate authority routing, registry, topology, active packet, allowed paths, and AGENTS/CLAUDE identity.
Run independent design, watcher/enforcement, and Ponytail/YAGNI adversarial audits.
Run negative probes for false readiness, forbidden old authority paths, unimplemented gates, absolute paths, and watcher correctness coupling.

attack_surfaces: A review finding is summarized away instead of integrated or explicitly rejected
YAGNI is misused to delete required parity or reliability scope
Architecture rules exist only as prose and drift from code
The watcher still builds while no longer understanding current product events or failure modes
Coarse initial crates become an accidental monolith or premature fine-grained crates recreate the original topology risk
New design authority paths drift across bootstrap, Codex, topology, registry, and task state
minimum_controls: Explicit scope-preservation and YAGNI applicability boundaries
Named executable conformance gates and exception policy
Watcher protocol handshake, coverage manifest, freshness tests, and release gate
Mechanically checked dependency directions plus evidence-based split criteria
Cross-file authority and ID invariant validation

execution_decomposition: Inspect and trace the complete v0.2.0 design and all peer reviews
Research Ponytail/YAGNI and current Rust architecture/watcher enforcement practices
Update canonical build rules and authority routing
Run structural, traceability, parse, invariant, and adversarial checks
